title.purpose: Page headers only (e.g. QR ทั้งหมด).

shadows.name: none
shadows.purpose: Flat by default. Depth is tonal step + 1px border, never box-shadow.

motion.name: ease-standard
motion.purpose: Default easing for color/border state transitions.

motion.name: duration-fast
motion.purpose: Hover, focus, and active state changes — users are in flow.

components.name: Primary Button
components.description: The single primary action per surface (create QR, confirm).
components.css: .ds-btn-primary { background: #f5f5f5; color: #0a0a0a; font: 500 0.875rem Inter, ui-sans-serif, system-ui, sans-serif; padding: 8px 16px; border: none; border-radius: 8px; transition: background 150ms cubic-bezier(0.4,0,0.2,1); } .ds-btn-primary:hover { background: #ffffff; } .ds-btn-primary:focus-visible { outline: none; box-shadow: 0 0 0 2px #0a0a0a, 0 0 0 4px rgba(16,185,129,0.7); }

components.name: Secondary Button
components.description: The workhorse action — edit, download, toggle, new folder.
components.css: .ds-btn-secondary { background: transparent; color: #f5f5f5; font: 400 0.75rem Inter, ui-sans-serif, system-ui, sans-serif; padding: 10px 12px; border: 1px solid #404040; border-radius: 8px; transition: border-color 150ms cubic-bezier(0.4,0,0.2,1); } .ds-btn-secondary:hover { border-color: #737373; } .ds-btn-secondary:focus-visible { outline: none; box-shadow: 0 0 0 2px #0a0a0a, 0 0 0 4px rgba(16,185,129,0.7); }

components.name: Danger Button
components.css: .ds-btn-danger { background: transparent; color: #f87171; font: 400 0.75rem Inter, ui-sans-serif, system-ui, sans-serif; padding: 10px 12px; border: 1px solid rgba(127,29,29,0.5); border-radius: 8px; transition: border-color 150ms, color 150ms; } .ds-btn-danger:hover { border-color: #b91c1c; color: #fca5a5; } .ds-btn-danger:focus-visible { outline: none; box-shadow: 0 0 0 2px #0a0a0a, 0 0 0 4px rgba(16,185,129,0.7); }

components.name: Input
components.description: Text field — surface fill, border-lightens focus, no glow.
components.html: <input class="ds-input" placeholder="ชื่อโฟลเดอร์ / แบรนด์" />
components.css: .ds-input { background: #171717; color: #f5f5f5; font: 400 0.875rem Inter, ui-sans-serif, system-ui, sans-serif; padding: 10px 16px; border: 1px solid #404040; border-radius: 8px; transition: border-color 150ms cubic-bezier(0.4,0,0.2,1); } .ds-input::placeholder { color: #737373; } .ds-input:focus { outline: none; border-color: #a3a3a3; }

components.name: Folder Tile
components.description: Brand folder — navigates on click, highlights emerald as a drop target.
components.css: .ds-folder { display: flex; align-items: center; gap: 12px; padding: 12px 16px; background: transparent; border: 1px solid #262626; border-radius: 12px; text-align: left; cursor: pointer; transition: border-color 150ms, background 150ms; } .ds-folder:hover { border-color: #525252; } .ds-folder.is-drop-target { border-color: #10b981; background: rgba(16,185,129,0.1); } .ds-folder-icon { font-size: 20px; } .ds-folder-name { display: block; font: 400 0.875rem Inter, sans-serif; color: #f5f5f5; } .ds-folder-count { display: block; font: 400 0.75rem Inter, sans-serif; color: #737373; }

components.name: Status Chip
components.description: QR active/inactive — active glows emerald, inactive recedes.
components.css: .ds-chip { display: inline-block; font: 400 0.75rem Inter, sans-serif; padding: 2px 8px; border-radius: 9999px; } .ds-chip.is-active { background: rgba(6,78,59,0.4); color: #34d399; } .ds-chip.is-inactive { background: #262626; color: #737373; }

narrative.overview: A control surface, not a showcase. A marketing team opens it between other tasks to spin up a short link or QR, repoint a campaign, read a click trend, arrange a brand's bio page, and drag a link into a brand folder — then leaves. Exception: the public bio page (/p/{handle}) is brand surface — mobile-first, themed per brand — while its editor stays inside this utility panel. Everything is dark, flat, and border-defined: a near-black canvas with content lifted one tonal step onto surfaces, separated by hairline borders. No gradient, no shadow, no decorative motion. The one voice of color is Signal Green (emerald), used only where state lives, never as decoration. Type is a single family (Inter) at a fixed rem scale; monospace shows up only for machine tokens like a /r/{code} short link. Components are tactile and confident — real hover and focus feedback, honest press states — restrained in ornament, not in responsiveness.
keyCharacteristics: Dark, flat, border-defined — depth from tone + 1px lines, never shadow
Emerald is a state signal on a handful of elements per screen, never decoration
One type family (Inter), fixed rem scale, mono only for codes
Thai-first UX copy: direct, short, immediately legible
WCAG AA legibility is a floor, not a nice-to-have

rules.name: The Accent-as-Signal Rule
rules.body: Emerald is reserved for state — active, selected, drop-target, focus. If it is doing decoration, it is wrong. Its rarity is the point.

rules.name: The Legibility-Floor Rule
rules.body: No meaningful text uses neutral-600 (#525252) on canvas — it fails AA (~2.6:1). Muted text bottoms out at neutral-500 (#737373); prefer neutral-400 when the text carries real information.

rules.name: The One-Family Rule
rules.body: Inter carries everything. A second UI face is prohibited; if two heading styles look different across screens, one is a bug.

rules.name: The Fixed-Scale Rule
rules.body: Type sizes are fixed rem steps, never fluid clamp(). A product tool viewed at consistent DPI does not need fluid headings.

rules.name: The Flat-By-Default Rule
rules.body: No box-shadow, ever, for elevation. Hierarchy is border + tonal step. To feel raised, lighten the border or step the background one tone — do not float it.

rules.name: The State-Not-Ornament Rule
rules.body: The only things that visibly change are responses to the user: border lightens on hover, an emerald ring on focus, an emerald tint on a live drop target. Nothing moves or glows for decoration.

dos: Do keep emerald a state signal — active, selected, drop target, focus — on only a handful of elements per screen.
Do build depth from 1px borders + the canvas→surface tonal step (#0a0a0a → #171717), never shadow.
Do set every type element in Inter; reserve monospace for machine tokens like /r/{code}.
Do hold WCAG AA: meaningful text at neutral-500 (#737373) minimum on canvas, neutral-400 when it carries information; placeholders at #737373, not dimmer.
Do give every interactive element a visible emerald focus-visible ring, and keep one clear primary action per surface.
donts: Don't ship the SaaS / AI-generic look: identical card grids wall to wall, gradient fills, gradient text, the hero-metric template, tiny uppercase tracked eyebrows over every section, or decorative glassmorphism.
Don't let a surface get cluttered or over-dense — crowded actions, buried hierarchy, no obvious next step. If a screen has more than one primary action, one is wrong.
Don't use neutral-600 (#525252) for any readable text on canvas — it fails AA (~2.6:1).
Don't put gray text on the emerald chip or any colored fill; use the fill's own hue (emerald-400 on emerald-900).
Don't add side-stripe borders (border-left/border-right > 1px as an accent) or any box-shadow for elevation.
